demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged care facility

contact_nok.relationship: residential aged care nursing staff (calling on the resident's behalf)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call placed by aged care nursing staff) 'He was completely normal this morning and now he's fighting us and making no sense — he's shouting that the walls are melting and that dogs are biting his legs, and he's trying to hit anyone who comes near him. This came on out of nowhere over the last few hours.' (The resident, when he speaks, calls out: 'The walls are melting, and the dogs are biting my legs, make them stop!')
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Nursing staff are ringing the after-hours telehealth line because a normally lucid resident has suddenly become severely confused, agitated and is seeing things that are not there, and they are not sure what to do or whether he needs to be seen tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden, over the last several hours this afternoon and evening — he was his normal self at breakfast
symptom_narrative.character: staff say he keeps drifting between being very drowsy and being wildly agitated; he can't hold a conversation or stay on a topic, his speech is rambling and doesn't make sense, and he keeps picking at the air and saying he can see animals in the room
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst they have ever seen him — combative, trying to strike staff, and terrified of the things he is seeing
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: not applicable — a whole-person change in his behaviour and awareness
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes and goes; worse this evening, and his normal day-night sleep pattern has completely reversed
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; a bright, loud, busy room seems to wind him up further
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the loud television, bright lights and several staff talking to him at once make the agitation worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: staff report he feels hot and looks sweaty and flushed; he is more distractible and inattentive than they have ever seen
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: none that staff can recall — they describe him as normally alert and oriented
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally walks with a frame and converses logically; right now he cannot be safely left alone, cannot follow instructions and is at high risk of falling or hurting himself or staff

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged care facility with nursing staff on site; normally mobilises with a walking frame and is described as usually lucid and sociable

objective_data_offered.value: 38.2 degrees on the facility's ear thermometer
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading taken by facility staff, not clinician-verified
carer-read from the facility observation machine while the resident was agitated

objective_data_offered.value: breathing fast, staff counted about 22 breaths a minute

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: finger-probe reading on a moving, agitated patient

objective_data_offered.value: on the video the resident looks flushed and sweaty, is picking at the air, is very distractible and is calling out at things in the room; he attempts to strike staff who approach
